title: Why Naked Mole-Rats Almost Never Get Cancer
summary: Naked mole-rats show extraordinary cancer resistance through unusual cell-contact controls, high-molecular-mass hyaluronan, protein stability, long-lived tissues, and efficient stress responses.

lesson.learn: Naked mole-rats are not absolutely cancer-proof, but spontaneous cancer is remarkably rare. Their cells behave unusually cautiously when crowded or stressed. A famous mechanism involves high-molecular-mass hyaluronan, a large molecule in the extracellular matrix that helps cells sense crowding and stop dividing early. They also show unusual stress tolerance, protein maintenance, and long-lived tissue stability. These defenses fit an animal that lives underground in low-oxygen colonies and can survive for decades. The lesson is not one miracle gene, but overlapping safeguards that make uncontrolled cell growth unusually difficult.
lesson.simpler: Naked mole-rats rarely get cancer because their cells are unusually good at stopping risky growth and surviving stress without becoming tumors.
lesson.keyTakeaway: Naked mole-rat cancer resistance appears to come from several overlapping safeguards, especially early growth arrest and high-molecular-mass hyaluronan that help prevent damaged cells from becoming tumors.

learnImageCredit.creator: BFS Man from Webster, TX, USA

lesson.deeperDive: The best-known discovery showed that naked mole-rat cells secrete unusually large hyaluronan molecules and stop dividing when they contact neighboring cells. Removing that molecule made cells more vulnerable to transformation in experiments, suggesting a direct role in tumor suppression. Later work added nuance: DNA repair, protein quality control, immune surveillance, metabolism, and tissue environment may all contribute. A few cancer cases have been reported, so the accurate claim is exceptional resistance rather than impossibility. Studying the species may help cancer and aging research, but copying the effect in humans is not straightforward because the protection belongs to a whole biological system.
lesson.funFact: Naked mole-rats can live more than 30 years, far longer than most rodents their size.

East Africa - native region of naked mole-rats

years: 2013 - Nature paper links high-molecular-mass hyaluronan to cancer resistance
2016 - rare cancer cases in naked mole-rats are reported
2020s - research broadens to protein stability and immune mechanisms
Present day - naked mole-rats remain a major model for longevity biology
concepts: High-molecular-mass hyaluronan - large matrix molecule linked to tumor resistance
Contact inhibition - stopping cell division when cells become crowded
Tumor suppression - mechanisms that prevent cancer formation
Longevity - unusually long lifespan relative to body size
